seoTitle: Faratronic Film Capacitors Product Catalog | LiTong Electronics
seoDescription: Browse Faratronic film capacitors, DC-Link capacitors, EMI suppression capacitors, and automotive capacitors. Technical specifications, selection guide, and alternative parts.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Faratronic capacitor for my application?
faqs.answer: Consider these key parameters: rated voltage (1.5-2x operating voltage), capacitance value, ripple current requirements, operating temperature range, physical dimensions, and application type (industrial, automotive, consumer).
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your application specifications for personalized capacitor selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between X2 and Y2 capacitors?
faqs.answer: X2 capacitors are used across the line (line-to-neutral) for EMI filtering and are rated for 250V AC. Y2 capacitors are used line-to-ground for safety applications and provide reinforced insulation with higher impulse withstand voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use X2 for normal mode EMI filtering across AC lines. Use Y2 for line-to-ground applications where safety isolation is required.

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for DC-Link capacitors?
faqs.answer: DC-Link capacitors are used in inverter DC bus circuits to smooth the DC voltage, absorb ripple current, and provide energy storage. Common applications include motor drives, solar inverters, EV charging systems, and UPS systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: For inverter applications, select DC-Link capacitors with sufficient voltage margin, high ripple current capability, and low ESR for your switching frequency.

faqs.question: Are Faratronic capacitors RoHS compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Faratronic capacitors are RoHS compliant and free from lead, mercury, cadmium, and other restricted substances. Certificates of compliance are available upon request.
faqs.decisionGuide: For RoHS compliance documentation, contact our sales team with your specific part numbers.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for Faratronic capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard products typically have 4-6 weeks lead time. High-volume or custom products may require 8-12 weeks. We maintain inventory of popular models for faster delivery.
faqs.decisionGuide: For urgent requirements, check our stock availability or discuss scheduled orders with our sales team.

categories.name: Film Capacitors
categories.description: High-performance metallized film capacitors for DC-Link, snubber, and filtering applications
categories.longDescription: Faratronic film capacitors utilize advanced metallized film technology to deliver exceptional performance in power electronics applications. These capacitors feature self-healing properties, high ripple current capability, low ESR/ESL, and long operational lifetime. Available in various dielectric materials (PP, PET) and configurations for DC-Link, snubber, and filtering applications in industrial, automotive, and renewable energy systems. Contact LiTong Electronics, your authorized Faratronic distributor, for capacitor selection guidance.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on voltage rating, capacitance, ripple current, and application type. DC-Link capacitors for inverter buses, snubber capacitors for IGBT protection.

faqs.question: What dielectric materials are used in Faratronic film capacitors?
faqs.answer: Faratronic uses polypropylene (PP) film for high-frequency and pulse applications due to its low losses, and polyester (PET) film for general-purpose applications. PP film is preferred for DC-Link and snubber applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-frequency switching applications, choose PP film capacitors. For cost-sensitive general applications, PET film may be suitable.

faqs.question: What is self-healing in metallized film capacitors?
faqs.answer: Self-healing is a property where the capacitor can clear small dielectric breakdowns by vaporizing the thin metallization around the fault point, isolating the defect and allowing continued operation. This prevents catastrophic failure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Self-healing makes metallized film capacitors highly reliable for critical applications. Specify metallized film for applications requiring high reliability.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the required capacitance for a DC-Link application?
faqs.answer: DC-Link capacitance depends on allowable voltage ripple, load current, and switching frequency. Typical formula: C = I / (2 × f × ΔV), where I is current, f is frequency, and ΔV is allowable voltage ripple.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your inverter specifications for detailed DC-Link capacitor sizing calculations.

faqs.question: What is the typical ESR of Faratronic film capacitors?
faqs.answer: ESR varies by capacitance and voltage rating, typically ranging from 1mΩ to 50mΩ. Lower ESR is achieved with larger capacitance values and optimized internal connections.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-frequency applications, select capacitors with lowest ESR specifications. Check datasheet for specific ESR values at your operating frequency.

faqs.question: Can film capacitors be used in series for higher voltage?
faqs.answer: Yes, film capacitors can be connected in series to achieve higher voltage ratings. However, voltage balancing resistors are required to ensure equal voltage distribution across each capacitor.
faqs.decisionGuide: For series connection, use matched capacitors and implement voltage balancing. Contact our FAE team for series connection design guidance.

faqs.question: What is the temperature coefficient of film capacitors?
faqs.answer: Polypropylene film capacitors have a negative temperature coefficient of approximately -200 ppm/°C. Capacitance decreases slightly with increasing temperature but remains very stable compared to other capacitor types.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring tight capacitance tolerance over temperature, PP film capacitors are an excellent choice due to their stability.

products.name: DC-Link Capacitor 20uF 600V
products.shortDescription: High-performance DC-Link capacitor with 20uF capacitance and 600V rating for inverter applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The C3D3K205K6AHA01 is a metallized polypropylene DC-Link capacitor designed for high-power inverter applications. It features a robust cylindrical aluminum case with M8 threaded terminals for reliable high-current connections.

This capacitor delivers exceptional ripple current capability of up to 15A at 70°C, making it ideal for demanding motor drive and solar inverter applications. The self-healing metallized film technology ensures long operational lifetime exceeding 100,000 hours at rated conditions.

With low ESR of less than 5mΩ and ESL below 30nH, this capacitor effectively filters high-frequency ripple in DC bus circuits. The 600V DC rating provides ample voltage margin for 380-480V AC inverter applications.
features: Low ESR and ESL for high frequency
products.faeReview: The C3D3K205K6AHA01 is my go-to recommendation for 5-10kW inverter applications. The 20uF/600V rating hits the sweet spot for 380-480V motor drives. In my experience, the actual ripple current capability often exceeds the datasheet rating by 10-15% due to Faratronic's conservative specifications. The M8 terminals are a key advantage - they maintain reliable contact under thermal cycling compared to snap-in terminals. For solar inverters, I recommend operating at 80% of rated voltage to extend lifetime significantly. The bottom stud mounting makes installation straightforward in compact inverter designs.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower capacitance alternative for lower power applications
alternativeParts.comparison: 15uF vs 20uF, same voltage rating and form factor
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for 3-7kW inverter applications where 15uF capacitance is sufficient

alternativeParts.reason: Higher capacitance for better ripple filtering
alternativeParts.comparison: 25uF vs 20uF, same voltage rating, slightly larger size
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring lower voltage ripple or higher power

companionParts.description: Snubber capacitor 1000pF 600V for IGBT protection
DC-Link capacitor 4.7uF 600V for auxiliary circuits
High-capacitance DC-Link 33uF 600V for larger inverters

applicationScenarios.description: DC-Link capacitor for 7.5kW VFD with 380V AC input
applicationScenarios.configuration: Single capacitor per phase, 540V DC bus

applicationScenarios.description: DC input filtering for 5kW residential solar inverter

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for this capacitor?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 15A at 70°C and 10kHz frequency. At lower temperatures, higher ripple current is permissible. Refer to the derating curves in the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 15A ripple, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or select a higher capacitance model.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used in outdoor applications?
faqs.answer: The capacitor is rated for -40°C to +105°C operation. For outdoor applications, additional enclosure protection is recommended to prevent moisture ingress and UV degradation.
faqs.decisionGuide: For outdoor installations, use an IP65-rated enclosure and consider temperature derating for extreme climates.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime at 85°C operation?
faqs.answer: Lifetime follows the Arrhenius equation. At 85°C, expected lifetime is approximately 50,000 hours. Every 10°C reduction doubles the lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum lifetime, operate at lowest practical temperature and voltage. Consider forced air cooling for high-temperature environments.

faqs.question: Is this capacitor suitable for EV charging applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, this capacitor is suitable for Level 2 EV charging stations. The 600V rating and high ripple current capability meet typical 7-22kW charger requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For EV charging, verify the specific voltage and ripple requirements of your design. Contact our FAE team for EV application support.

faqs.question: What mounting orientation is recommended?
faqs.answer: Vertical mounting with terminals up is recommended for optimal heat dissipation. Horizontal mounting is acceptable but may require slight derating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Mount vertically in well-ventilated areas. Ensure adequate clearance around the capacitor for heat dissipation.

products.name: DC-Link Capacitor 4.7uF 600V
products.shortDescription: Compact DC-Link capacitor with 4.7uF capacitance for auxiliary power circuits and smaller inverters.
descriptionParagraphs: The C3D3K475K6AHA01 is a compact metallized polypropylene DC-Link capacitor designed for auxiliary power circuits and smaller inverter applications. Its cylindrical aluminum case with snap-in terminals enables easy PCB mounting.

This capacitor provides 8A ripple current capability at 70°C, suitable for 1-3kW inverter applications and power supply DC bus filtering. The self-healing film technology ensures reliable operation over the 100,000 hour rated lifetime.
With dimensions of 35mm diameter by 50mm length, this capacitor fits in space-constrained designs while maintaining excellent electrical performance with ESR below 10mΩ.
products.faeReview: The C3D3K475K6AHA01 is perfect for auxiliary power supplies and smaller inverters in the 1-3kW range. I frequently recommend this part for LED driver applications where space is limited but reliability is critical. The snap-in terminals make assembly straightforward on automated production lines. One tip: the 4.7uF value works well for PFC output filtering in 500-1000W power supplies. For higher reliability, I suggest running at 450-500V DC rather than the full 600V rating - this can extend lifetime by 30-40%. The compact 35mm diameter is a key advantage in space-constrained designs.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 3.3uF provides sufficient filtering
Use for applications requiring lower ripple voltage

companionParts.description: DC-Link capacitor 20uF 600V for main power stage
X2 EMI suppression capacitor for input filtering
Snubber capacitor 470pF for switch protection

applicationScenarios.description: PFC output capacitor for 200W LED driver
applicationScenarios.configuration: Single capacitor after PFC boost stage

faqs.question: What is the lead spacing for the snap-in terminals?
faqs.answer: The snap-in terminals have 10mm lead spacing, compatible with standard PCB hole patterns for snap-in capacitors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your PCB layout uses 10mm hole spacing for proper fit. Contact our FAE team for mechanical drawings.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used for PFC output filtering?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 4.7uF/600V rating is well-suited for PFC output filtering in 500-1500W power supplies. The low ESR helps minimize switching losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: For PFC applications, verify the hold-up time and ripple current requirements match this capacitor's specifications.

faqs.question: What is the insulation resistance of this capacitor?
faqs.answer: Insulation resistance exceeds 30,000 MΩ at 20°C and 100V DC test voltage, indicating excellent dielectric quality.
faqs.decisionGuide: High insulation resistance ensures low leakage current, important for standby power requirements.

faqs.question: Is this capacitor vibration resistant?
faqs.answer: The capacitor meets IEC 60068-2-6 vibration test standards, suitable for industrial applications with moderate vibration levels.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-vibration environments, ensure proper mechanical mounting and consider additional support.

faqs.question: What is the dissipation factor at 1kHz?
faqs.answer: The dissipation factor (tan δ) is typically less than 0.001 (0.1%) at 1kHz and 20°C, indicating very low losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low dissipation factor means minimal self-heating, allowing higher ripple current capability.

categories.name: EMI Suppression Capacitors
categories.description: X and Y class safety capacitors for electromagnetic interference filtering
categories.longDescription: Faratronic EMI suppression capacitors are safety-rated capacitors designed for electromagnetic interference filtering in AC power applications. X class capacitors are used across the line (line-to-neutral) while Y class capacitors provide line-to-ground isolation. These capacitors meet international safety standards including IEC 60384-14 and UL 60384-14, with X2 (250V AC) and Y2 (250V AC) ratings available. Contact LiTong Electronics for EMI capacitor selection guidance.
categories.selectionGuide: Select X2 capacitors for line-to-line filtering, Y2 capacitors for line-to-ground. Choose capacitance based on required attenuation and frequency.

faqs.question: What is the difference between X2 and Y2 capacitors?
faqs.answer: X2 capacitors are used across the line (line-to-neutral) for normal mode EMI filtering. Y2 capacitors are used line-to-ground and must provide reinforced insulation for safety. Y2 capacitors have stricter impulse voltage requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use X2 for differential mode filtering across AC lines. Use Y2 for common mode filtering to ground where safety isolation is required.

faqs.question: What safety standards do Faratronic EMI capacitors meet?
faqs.answer: Faratronic EMI capacitors are certified to IEC 60384-14, UL 60384-14, EN 60384-14, and CQC standards. They carry UL, VDE, and ENEC safety approvals.
faqs.decisionGuide: For international products, specify capacitors with multiple safety approvals. Check specific certifications for your target markets.

faqs.question: Can X2 capacitors be used for DC applications?
faqs.answer: While X2 capacitors are rated for AC, they can be used in DC applications at higher voltages (typically 630V DC for X2 275V AC rated parts). Check the datasheet for DC rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: For DC applications, verify the DC voltage rating in the datasheet. DC rating is typically 2.3x the AC rating.

faqs.question: What is the typical capacitance range for EMI suppression capacitors?
faqs.answer: X2 capacitors typically range from 0.01uF to 10uF. Y2 capacitors are typically 100pF to 4700pF due to safety limitations on line-to-ground capacitance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select capacitance based on required filter attenuation. Higher capacitance provides better low-frequency filtering but increases leakage current for Y caps.

faqs.question: Are Faratronic EMI capacitors flame retardant?
faqs.answer: Yes, Faratronic EMI capacitors use flame retardant materials meeting UL 94 V-0 requirements. The encapsulation and case materials are self-extinguishing.
faqs.decisionGuide: For safety-critical applications, verify flame retardant ratings. All Faratronic safety capacitors meet stringent fire safety requirements.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of EMI suppression capacitors?
faqs.answer: EMI capacitors are typically rated for 100,000 hours at rated voltage and 85°C. Actual lifetime depends on operating voltage, temperature, and humidity conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For long lifetime, operate below rated voltage and temperature. Avoid high-humidity environments without proper protection.

products.name: X2 EMI Capacitor 0.47uF 275V AC
products.shortDescription: X2 class metallized polypropylene capacitor for across-the-line EMI filtering applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MKP-X2-0.47uF-275V is a metallized polypropylene X2 class safety capacitor designed for electromagnetic interference filtering across AC power lines. It features self-healing properties and meets international safety standards.

This capacitor provides 0.47uF capacitance with 275V AC rating, suitable for 220-240V mains applications. The compact box-style package with 15mm lead spacing enables easy PCB mounting in power supply designs.

With excellent self-healing characteristics and stable capacitance over temperature, this capacitor delivers reliable EMI suppression for the full product lifetime. The polypropylene dielectric provides low losses and high pulse capability.
features: X2 safety rated for across-line use
products.faeReview: The MKP-X2-0.47uF is my standard recommendation for 200-300W power supply input filtering. The 0.47uF value provides excellent attenuation in the 100kHz-1MHz range where switch-mode noise is most problematic. I always verify that customers understand X2 capacitors are for line-to-line only - never use them for line-to-ground applications. The 275V AC rating is conservative for 230V mains, providing good margin for voltage transients. One design tip: place X2 capacitors as close to the AC input connector as possible for maximum effectiveness.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for lower power supplies where 0.33uF provides sufficient filtering

alternativeParts.reason: Higher capacitance for better low-frequency attenuation
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring better attenuation below 150kHz

companionParts.description: Y2 capacitor 2200pF for line-to-ground filtering
DC-Link capacitor for PFC output
Common mode choke for complete EMI filter

applicationScenarios.description: X2 capacitor for differential mode filtering in switch-mode power supply
applicationScenarios.configuration: Across L and N lines after fuse and before bridge rectifier
Parallel with common mode choke for complete EMI solution

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used for 120V AC applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 275V AC rating is suitable for both 120V and 240V AC applications. The higher voltage rating provides additional safety margin for 120V systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: For universal input power supplies (90-264V AC), X2 275V capacitors are the standard choice.

faqs.question: What is the pulse capability of this capacitor?
faqs.answer: The capacitor can withstand high peak pulse currents typical of switch-mode power supply applications. The exact rating depends on pulse waveform and repetition rate.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications with high inrush currents, verify pulse capability with our FAE team.

faqs.question: Is this capacitor suitable for outdoor lighting applications?
faqs.answer: The -40°C to +110°C temperature range suits outdoor applications. Additional enclosure protection is recommended for direct exposure to moisture.
faqs.decisionGuide: For outdoor LED drivers, ensure adequate IP rating of the complete fixture to protect the capacitor.

faqs.question: What is the capacitance tolerance over temperature?
faqs.answer: Capacitance change is within ±10% from -40°C to +110°C, with typical drift of -2% to +5% over the temperature range.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring tight capacitance tolerance, consider temperature effects in the design.

faqs.question: Can multiple capacitors be paralleled for higher capacitance?
faqs.answer: Yes, X2 capacitors can be paralleled to achieve higher capacitance values. Ensure proper PCB layout to minimize inductance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For parallel connection, keep traces short and symmetrical to maintain balanced current sharing.

products.name: Y2 EMI Capacitor 2200pF 250V AC
products.shortDescription: Y2 class metallized polypropylene capacitor for line-to-ground EMI filtering with safety isolation.
descriptionParagraphs: The MKP-Y2-2200pF-250V is a Y2 class safety capacitor designed for line-to-ground electromagnetic interference filtering. It provides reinforced insulation and meets stringent safety requirements for touch-safe applications.

With 2200pF capacitance and 250V AC rating, this capacitor effectively suppresses common-mode noise while maintaining safety isolation. The compact disc-style construction with 10mm lead spacing fits standard PCB layouts.
The self-healing metallized polypropylene film ensures long-term reliability. All Y2 capacitors undergo 100% testing for safety compliance including impulse voltage and insulation resistance.
products.faeReview: The MKP-Y2-2200pF is the most common Y2 capacitor I specify for general-purpose power supplies. The 2200pF value provides good high-frequency common-mode attenuation while keeping leakage current low. Remember that Y2 capacitors are for line-to-ground ONLY - using them line-to-line violates safety standards. The 5kV impulse rating is critical for handling lightning-induced transients. One important consideration: total Y capacitance per device is limited by leakage current regulations - typically 3300pF max for portable equipment. Always calculate total leakage current when using multiple Y caps.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower capacitance for lower leakage current
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for medical or portable equipment with strict leakage limits
Use for stationary equipment where leakage current is less critical

companionParts.description: X2 capacitor for differential mode filtering
High inductance CM choke for enhanced filtering
Safety discharge resistor for X capacitor

applicationScenarios.description: Y2 capacitor for line-to-ground filtering in switch-mode power supply
applicationScenarios.configuration: Between L-GND and N-GND after common mode choke

applicationScenarios.description: Ground filtering for Class I appliance
applicationScenarios.configuration: Parallel with X2 capacitor for complete EMI solution

faqs.question: What is the maximum leakage current with this capacitor?
faqs.answer: At 250V AC 50Hz, leakage current is approximately 0.35mA. Total device leakage must comply with safety standards (typically <0.25mA for Class II, <3.5mA for Class I).
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total leakage current for all Y capacitors in your design. Consider using lower capacitance if leakage limits are strict.

faqs.question: Can Y2 capacitors fail short circuit?
faqs.answer: Y2 capacitors are designed with self-healing properties and should not fail short under normal conditions. Safety standards require that any failure be open-circuit or high-resistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For critical safety applications, specify Y2 capacitors from reputable manufacturers with full safety certifications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Y1 and Y2 capacitors?
faqs.answer: Y1 capacitors are rated for 500V AC and provide basic insulation. Y2 capacitors are rated for 250V AC and provide reinforced or double insulation. Y2 is more common in 230V applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 230V AC applications, Y2 capacitors are typically sufficient. Use Y1 only if specifically required by your safety agency.

faqs.question: How do I test Y2 capacitors for safety compliance?
faqs.answer: Y2 capacitors require impulse voltage testing at 5kV and AC voltage testing at 1500V. These tests verify insulation integrity and are performed during manufacturing.
faqs.decisionGuide: For incoming inspection, verify capacitance, dissipation factor, and insulation resistance. Full safety testing requires specialized equipment.

faqs.question: Can Y2 capacitors be used in series for higher voltage?
faqs.answer: No, Y2 capacitors should not be used in series for voltage sharing. Safety standards require each Y capacitor to be rated for the full line-to-ground voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: For higher voltage applications, select appropriately rated Y capacitors rather than series connection.

categories.name: Automotive Capacitors
categories.description: AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors for automotive electronics and EV applications
categories.longDescription: Faratronic automotive capacitors are specifically designed and qualified for automotive electronics applications. All automotive-grade capacitors meet AEC-Q200 reliability standards and are suitable for use in electric vehicles, hybrid vehicles, and traditional automotive electronics. These capacitors feature enhanced robustness for harsh automotive environments including wide temperature ranges, high vibration resistance, and long operational lifetime. Contact LiTong Electronics, your authorized Faratronic distributor, for automotive capacitor selection.
categories.selectionGuide: Select AEC-Q200 qualified parts for automotive applications. Consider temperature grade, vibration resistance, and lifetime requirements for under-hood applications.

faqs.question: What is AEC-Q200 qualification?
faqs.answer: AEC-Q200 is the automotive electronics council standard for passive components. It defines stress test qualification requirements including temperature cycling, humidity, vibration, and mechanical shock testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, always specify AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors to ensure reliability in harsh automotive environments.

faqs.question: What temperature grades are available for automotive capacitors?
faqs.answer: Automotive capacitors are available in Grade 1 (-40°C to +125°C), Grade 2 (-40°C to +105°C), and Grade 3 (-40°C to +85°C). Grade 1 is required for under-hood applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select Grade 1 for engine compartment applications. Grade 2 is suitable for cabin and chassis applications.

faqs.question: Are Faratronic automotive capacitors suitable for EV applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Faratronic offers capacitors specifically designed for EV applications including DC-Link capacitors for onboard chargers, DC-DC converters, and motor drives. These meet both AEC-Q200 and specific EV requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For EV applications, contact our FAE team for recommendations on capacitors qualified for high voltage and high ripple current EV applications.

faqs.question: What vibration standards do automotive capacitors meet?
faqs.answer: Automotive capacitors are tested to meet AEC-Q200 vibration requirements including random vibration (5-2000Hz, 8 hours per axis) and mechanical shock (100G, 6ms half-sine).
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-vibration applications like chassis or wheel-well mounting, verify specific vibration test data for your application.

faqs.question: What documentation is provided with automotive capacitors?
faqs.answer: Automotive capacitors come with full PPAP documentation including material certifications, test reports, process flow diagrams, and control plans. AEC-Q200 test reports are standard.
faqs.decisionGuide: For production automotive programs, request full PPAP documentation early in the qualification process.

faqs.question: Can automotive capacitors be used in commercial vehicles?
faqs.answer: Yes, AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors are suitable for commercial vehicles, trucks, and heavy equipment. The qualification is often more stringent than commercial vehicle requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For commercial vehicle applications, AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors provide excellent reliability. Contact us for specific commercial vehicle recommendations.

products.name: Automotive DC-Link Capacitor 10uF 900V
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q200 Grade 1 DC-Link capacitor for automotive onboard chargers and DC-DC converters.
descriptionParagraphs: The C3A3K106K9AHA01 is an AEC-Q200 Grade 1 qualified DC-Link capacitor specifically designed for automotive power electronics. With 10uF capacitance and 900V DC rating, it is ideal for 400V EV onboard chargers and DC-DC converters.

This capacitor features enhanced robustness for automotive environments including -40°C to +125°C temperature range, high vibration resistance, and 20A ripple current capability. The self-healing film technology ensures long lifetime in demanding automotive applications.

All automotive-grade capacitors undergo 100% screening including high-voltage testing, thermal cycling, and visual inspection. Full traceability and PPAP documentation are provided for automotive production programs.
products.faeReview: The C3A3K106K9AHA01 is my top recommendation for 400V EV onboard chargers. The 900V rating provides excellent margin for 400V battery systems, and the Grade 1 qualification ensures reliability in under-hood environments. I've used this part in several EV charger designs with excellent results. The 20A ripple current capability handles the high switching frequencies typical of SiC-based chargers. One key advantage: Faratronic provides complete PPAP documentation which is critical for automotive production. For 800V EV systems, consider the 1200V rated version. Always verify thermal management in your design - automotive under-hood temperatures can exceed 100°C.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for lower power chargers where 6.8uF is sufficient
Use for high-power chargers requiring lower ripple voltage

AEC-Q200 EMI capacitor for input filtering
Automotive snubber capacitor for IGBT protection

applicationScenarios.configuration: Single capacitor per phase in PFC output stage

faqs.question: What is the qualification process for AEC-Q200?
faqs.answer: AEC-Q200 qualification includes 17 test groups covering temperature cycling, humidity, vibration, mechanical shock, solderability, and electrical characterization. Each test group has specific acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: For new automotive designs, plan 3-6 months for capacitor qualification. Contact our FAE team for qualification support.

faqs.question: Is this capacitor suitable for 800V EV systems?
faqs.answer: The 900V rating is marginal for 800V systems which typically operate at 850-900V. For 800V applications, consider the 1200V rated version (C3A3K106K12AHA01).
faqs.decisionGuide: For 800V EV systems, use 1200V rated capacitors to ensure adequate voltage margin and long-term reliability.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime in automotive applications?
faqs.answer: At 105°C and rated voltage, lifetime is 100,000 hours. In typical automotive under-hood conditions (80-90°C), expected lifetime exceeds 200,000 hours.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended vehicle lifetime requirements, consider operating at reduced voltage and temperature.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor withstand automotive load dump conditions?
faqs.answer: The 900V rating provides margin for load dump transients which can reach 100-150V above nominal battery voltage. Additional TVS protection is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: For load dump protection, combine capacitor voltage rating with appropriate TVS diodes for complete protection.

faqs.question: What is the change control process for automotive capacitors?
faqs.answer: Automotive capacitors require PCN (Process Change Notification) per AEC-Q200. Customers receive advance notification of any process or material changes.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive production, ensure your supplier has robust change control processes. Faratronic provides 90-day PCN for automotive parts.

products.name: Automotive X2 Capacitor 0.47uF 275V
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q200 Grade 1 X2 capacitor for automotive EMI filtering applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MKP-AEC-0.47uF-275V is an AEC-Q200 Grade 1 qualified X2 capacitor specifically designed for automotive EMI filtering. It meets the stringent reliability requirements for automotive electronics while providing excellent EMI suppression performance.

With 0.47uF capacitance and 275V AC rating, this capacitor is suitable for 12V and 48V automotive electrical systems. The -40°C to +125°C temperature range ensures reliable operation in harsh automotive environments from cold start to under-hood conditions.

The capacitor features enhanced terminal strength for vibration resistance and meets all AEC-Q200 mechanical and environmental test requirements. Full automotive documentation including PPAP and material declarations is provided.
products.faeReview: The MKP-AEC-0.47uF is my standard recommendation for automotive ECU EMI filtering. The AEC-Q200 Grade 1 qualification gives confidence for any automotive application, and the X2 rating ensures safety compliance. I've specified this part in numerous automotive LED driver and ECU designs. The enhanced terminal strength is important - standard commercial capacitors can fail in automotive vibration testing. One tip: for 48V mild-hybrid systems, this capacitor's 275V AC rating provides excellent margin. Always verify the complete AEC-Q200 test report for your specific application requirements. Faratronic's automotive documentation package is comprehensive, making PPAP submission straightforward.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 0.33uF provides sufficient filtering
Use for applications requiring better low-frequency EMI attenuation

faqs.question: Is this capacitor suitable for 48V mild-hybrid systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 275V AC rating provides excellent margin for 48V systems. The capacitor can safely handle voltage transients typical of 48V automotive electrical systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 48V applications, this X2 capacitor provides both EMI filtering and adequate voltage margin for safe operation.

faqs.question: What is the difference between automotive and commercial grade X2 capacitors?
faqs.answer: Automotive grade capacitors undergo additional AEC-Q200 testing including extended temperature cycling, higher vibration levels, and more stringent mechanical stress tests. They also require full traceability and change control.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive production, always specify AEC-Q200 qualified parts. Commercial grade capacitors may not survive automotive reliability requirements.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used in safety-critical automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the X2 safety rating and AEC-Q200 qualification make this capacitor suitable for safety-critical applications. It meets the reliability and safety requirements for automotive use.
faqs.decisionGuide: For safety-critical applications, ensure complete documentation including FMEA and safety analysis is available.

faqs.question: What is the moisture sensitivity level of this capacitor?
faqs.answer: Automotive X2 capacitors are MSL 1 (unlimited floor life) due to their hermetic sealing and robust construction. No special handling is required.
faqs.decisionGuide: MSL 1 rating simplifies manufacturing - no baking or special storage required before soldering.

faqs.question: Are automotive capacitors more expensive than commercial grade?
faqs.answer: Yes, automotive grade capacitors typically cost 20-30% more due to additional testing, qualification, documentation, and traceability requirements. The cost is justified by the higher reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, the additional cost of AEC-Q200 qualified parts is necessary to ensure reliability and avoid field failures.

categories.name: Power Capacitors
categories.description: High-voltage power capacitors for industrial and renewable energy applications
categories.longDescription: Faratronic power capacitors are designed for high-voltage, high-power applications in industrial drives, renewable energy systems, and power quality equipment. These capacitors feature high capacitance values, high voltage ratings up to 3000V DC, and exceptional ripple current capability for demanding power electronics applications. Contact LiTong Electronics for power capacitor selection and distributor support.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on voltage, capacitance, and ripple current requirements. Higher voltage ratings provide margin for industrial applications with voltage transients.

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for power capacitors?
faqs.answer: Faratronic power capacitors are available from 600V DC to 3000V DC. Common ratings include 700V, 900V, 1100V, 1300V, and 2000V for various industrial and renewable energy applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select voltage rating at least 1.5x the nominal operating voltage to account for transients and ensure long lifetime.

faqs.question: What applications are power capacitors used for?
faqs.answer: Power capacitors are used in industrial motor drives, solar inverters, wind turbine converters, UPS systems, power factor correction equipment, and high-power welding equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-power applications (>10kW), select power capacitors with appropriate voltage and ripple current ratings for your specific application.

faqs.question: How do I calculate ripple current for power capacitor selection?
faqs.answer: Ripple current depends on load current, switching frequency, and DC bus voltage. For three-phase inverters: I_ripple ≈ 0.61 × I_DC. Always include margin for harmonic content.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your inverter specifications for detailed ripple current calculations and capacitor sizing.

faqs.question: What is the difference between power capacitors and standard film capacitors?
faqs.answer: Power capacitors feature larger case sizes, higher voltage ratings, higher ripple current capability, and enhanced thermal management. They are designed for continuous high-power operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications above 10kW or with high ripple current, select power capacitors rather than standard film capacitors.

faqs.question: Can power capacitors be used in series for higher voltage?
faqs.answer: Yes, power capacitors can be connected in series with voltage balancing resistors. This is common for high-voltage applications above 1500V DC.
faqs.decisionGuide: For series connection, use matched capacitors and implement proper voltage balancing. Contact our FAE team for series connection design support.

faqs.question: What cooling is required for power capacitors?
faqs.answer: Power capacitors may require forced air cooling or liquid cooling depending on power dissipation. Natural convection is sufficient for lower power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate power dissipation (P = I² × ESR) and ensure adequate cooling. Contact our FAE team for thermal design assistance.

products.name: Power Capacitor 50uF 1100V
products.shortDescription: High-power DC-Link capacitor with 50uF capacitance and 1100V rating for industrial drives and solar inverters.
descriptionParagraphs: The C3P3K506K11AHA01 is a high-power metallized polypropylene DC-Link capacitor designed for industrial motor drives and solar inverter applications. With 50uF capacitance and 1100V DC rating, it is ideal for 690V AC industrial drives and 1000V DC solar systems.

This capacitor delivers exceptional ripple current capability of up to 50A at 70°C, making it suitable for high-power applications up to 100kW. The cylindrical aluminum case with M12 threaded terminals ensures reliable high-current connections.

The self-healing film technology and robust construction provide 100,000 hour lifetime at rated conditions. The capacitor features low ESR (<2mΩ) and ESL (<20nH) for efficient high-frequency operation.
products.faeReview: The C3P3K506K11AHA01 is my go-to capacitor for 30-75kW industrial drives. The 50uF/1100V rating is perfect for 690V AC drives with regenerative capability. The 50A ripple current handles the high switching frequencies of modern IGBT and SiC inverters. I've used this part in numerous solar inverter designs with excellent field reliability. The M12 terminals are essential for high current - smaller terminals would overheat at 50A. One design recommendation: always use proper torque (15-20 Nm) on the M12 terminals to ensure low contact resistance. For thermal management, ensure at least 10mm clearance around the capacitor for air circulation.

alternativeParts.comparison: 40uF vs 50uF, same voltage and form factor
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for lower power drives where 40uF is sufficient
Use for high-power applications requiring lower ripple voltage

companionParts.description: Power capacitor 25uF 1100V for smaller drives
Snubber capacitor 1000pF 1100V for IGBT protection
Bleeder resistor for DC bus discharge

applicationScenarios.description: DC-Link capacitor for 690V AC industrial drive
applicationScenarios.configuration: Single capacitor for 1000V DC bus, 45kW continuous

applicationScenarios.description: DC input capacitor for 1000V string inverter

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for this capacitor?
faqs.answer: The capacitor is suitable for switching frequencies up to 50kHz. Above this frequency, ESL effects become significant. For higher frequencies, consider multiple smaller capacitors in parallel.
faqs.decisionGuide: For SiC-based inverters with >50kHz switching, use parallel capacitors to reduce effective ESL.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used for regenerative drives?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 1100V rating provides adequate margin for regenerative drives where DC bus voltage can rise during braking. The capacitor handles bidirectional ripple current.
faqs.decisionGuide: For regenerative applications, verify the maximum DC bus voltage during braking does not exceed capacitor rating.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime at 90°C operation?
faqs.answer: At 90°C, expected lifetime is approximately 70,000 hours. Lifetime doubles for every 10°C decrease in temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, ensure adequate cooling to keep capacitor temperature below 85°C.

faqs.question: Is forced air cooling required for this capacitor?
faqs.answer: Natural convection is sufficient for ripple currents up to 35A. For higher currents or elevated ambient temperatures, forced air cooling is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate thermal rise and implement cooling if capacitor surface temperature exceeds 85°C.

faqs.question: What is the discharge time constant with this capacitor?
faqs.answer: Discharge time depends on load resistance. With 100kΩ bleeder resistor, discharge to 37V (safe voltage) takes approximately 90 seconds from 1000V.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size bleeder resistors to meet safety discharge time requirements (typically <60 seconds to 50V).

products.name: High-Voltage Power Capacitor 20uF 2000V
products.shortDescription: Ultra-high voltage DC-Link capacitor with 20uF capacitance and 2000V rating for high-voltage industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The C3P3K206K20AHA01 is an ultra-high voltage metallized polypropylene DC-Link capacitor designed for high-voltage industrial drives and renewable energy systems. With 20uF capacitance and 2000V DC rating, it is suitable for 1500V DC systems and high-voltage motor drives.

This capacitor provides 35A ripple current capability at 70°C, suitable for high-power applications up to 150kW at elevated voltages. The robust cylindrical aluminum case with M12 terminals handles the high voltage and current requirements.

The capacitor features enhanced insulation systems for high-voltage operation and meets stringent industrial reliability standards. Self-healing film technology ensures long operational lifetime even under demanding conditions.
products.faeReview: The C3P3K206K20AHA01 is essential for 1500V solar inverters and high-voltage industrial drives. The 2000V rating provides good margin for 1500V systems which can see 1700V+ during transients. I've specified this part for several large-scale solar projects. The key design consideration is safety - 2000V requires proper clearances, creepage distances, and safety interlocks. The M12 terminals are nickel-plated for corrosion resistance which is important for outdoor solar applications. One tip: always use appropriately rated connectors and cables - standard terminals are not suitable for 2000V. For thermal management at high altitude solar sites, derate the ripple current by 10-15%.

alternativeParts.comparison: 15uF vs 20uF, same voltage and form factor
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 15uF provides sufficient filtering
Use for high-power applications requiring lower ripple voltage

Snubber capacitor for high-voltage IGBT protection
High-voltage bleeder resistor for safety discharge

applicationScenarios.description: DC bus capacitor for 1500V industrial drive

faqs.question: What safety precautions are required for 2000V capacitors?
faqs.answer: 2000V capacitors require proper safety clearances (minimum 25mm), safety interlocks, discharge circuits, and warning labels. Only qualified personnel should work on high-voltage equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow all applicable electrical safety standards and regulations for high-voltage equipment design and operation.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used at altitudes above 2000m?
faqs.answer: Standard capacitors are rated to 2000m altitude. For higher altitudes, voltage derating is required (typically 10% per 1000m above 2000m) due to reduced air insulation.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-altitude installations, contact our FAE team for altitude derating guidelines and recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the insulation resistance at 2000V?
faqs.answer: Insulation resistance exceeds 10,000 MΩ at 20°C when measured at 1000V DC test voltage. At operating voltage, leakage current is minimal.
faqs.decisionGuide: High insulation resistance ensures minimal power loss and reliable long-term operation.

faqs.question: Is partial discharge a concern with this capacitor?
faqs.answer: Faratronic power capacitors are designed and tested for low partial discharge (<10pC) at rated voltage. This ensures long-term reliability in high-voltage applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For critical high-voltage applications, specify capacitors with partial discharge testing and certification.

faqs.question: What is the series inductance and how does it affect performance?
faqs.answer: ESL is typically 20-25nH. At high switching frequencies (>20kHz), ESL can cause voltage spikes. Use multiple capacitors in parallel to reduce effective ESL.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-frequency applications, parallel multiple capacitors and minimize connection inductance with proper PCB layout.
